content.description:
A runtime debugging tool for identifying which items and views are responsible for rendered UI elements.

Instances of this type provide an interactive inspector interface with controls to enable debug mode, trigger element inspection, and view results showing the rendering chain for any clicked element.

## How It Works

1. Enable debug mode (adds `data-*` attributes to rendered elements during render)
2. Click "Inspect Element" to enter inspect mode
3. Hover over any element to highlight it, click to inspect
4. View the rendering chain: which item, view, and source code produced each element
5. Click links in the results to navigate directly to the responsible code

## See Also

- [element-inspector (library)](item://eeee0000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001) — the underlying library with `inspectOnce`, `collectElementInfo`, and `resolveAndNavigate` APIs
- [kernel:rendering-system](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000) — sets data attributes during render
- [Element Inspector](item://eeee0000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002) — the singleton instance
